title: phymatopsis digitata
intro: Phymatopsis digitata, also known as finger fern, is a type of fern native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Phymatopsis digitata include soothing skin irritations, treating wounds, and reducing fever. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
